title: Time-Ordered CatBoost CTR Specialist
summary: This dataset has very high-cardinality categorical structure (Driver, Race, Compound, Year) that the current frequency features likely underuse.
rationale: This dataset has very high-cardinality categorical structure (Driver, Race, Compound, Year) that the current frequency features likely underuse. CatBoost’s ordered categorical statistics and feature combinations are a strong adjacent-tabular pattern for exactly this regime, and `has_time` lets it respect row order instead of random permutations.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost classifier on mostly raw categorical columns plus the strongest numeric race-state features. Sort by `Race_Year`, `Driver`, `LapNumber`, set `has_time=True`, and let CatBoost learn combos such as `Race_Year x Driver`, `Race_Year x Compound x Stint`, and `LapBucket x Compound`. Compare standalone CatBoost and a simple rank blend with the current best model.
expected_effect: Better use of sparse strategy archetypes and driver/track interactions, with a plausible modest CV/LB gain if current models are underfitting categorical structure.
risk: If rows are not strictly time-sorted inside each driver-race, or if CV is random instead of grouped by Race_Year, the ordered statistics can still look better locally than they generalize.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
